title: An exploratory study of breathwork-induced altered states of consciousness in experienced practitioners: the airways to alteration (A2A) trial.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: Breathwork that increases ventilatory rate or depth represents an accessible non-pharmacological modality for potentially inducing altered states of consciousness (ASCs). Despite gaining traction as a potential therapeutic tool, empirical controlled research on breathwork and ASCs remains limited. Breathwork that increases ventilatory rate or depth represents an accessible non-pharmacological modality for potentially inducing altered states of consciousness (ASCs). Despite gaining traction as a potential therapeutic tool, empirical controlled research on breathwork and ASCs remains limited. METHODS: We examined the effects of a single session of high ventilation breathwork, compared to body scan meditation, in 24 healthy adults with primary outcomes of acute ASCs including mystical experience and emotional breakthrough. Sub-acute secondary outcomes were collected 1 week later. We examined the effects of a single session of high ventilation breathwork, compared to body scan

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Breathwork-induced altered states — systematic characterization of mystical-type experiences through voluntary hyperventilation

evaluation: Score 5: Excellent bridge (7 indicators) — Demonstrates that breathwork (a universal contemplative practice) induces mystical experiences with measurable phenomenological overlap with psychedelic states.
